_note: Curated Florida state officials (demo data). Shape is modeled loosely after OpenStates so we can swap in the live API later without changing the frontend. Names/districts reflect a demo-consistent snapshot of FL state government and may not perfectly track real-world changes.
_source: Manually curated — replace with OpenStates API or FL legislature feeds.

governor.name: Ron DeSantis
governor.bio: Ron DeSantis is the 46th Governor of Florida, serving since January 2019. Before that he represented Florida's 6th congressional district in the US House.

top_issues.name: Education
top_issues.stance: Supports school choice and parental rights in education.

top_issues.name: Public Safety
top_issues.stance: Prioritizes law enforcement and anti-crime initiatives.

top_issues.name: Tax Policy
top_issues.stance: Advocates for lower taxes and fiscal responsibility.

top_issues.name: Parental Rights
top_issues.stance: Supports parental authority in healthcare and education decisions.

governor.selection_detail: Elected statewide in November 2022 to a 4-year term.

contact.dc_office: Executive Office of the Governor, PL 05, The Capitol, 400 S Monroe St, Tallahassee, FL 32399-0001

lt_governor.name: Jay Collins
lt_governor.selection_detail: Appointed by Governor DeSantis on Feb 10, 2025 after Jeanette Nuñez resigned to become interim president of Florida International University. The Lt. Governor is normally elected on the same ticket as the Governor.

contact.dc_office: Office of the Lieutenant Governor, PL 04, The Capitol, 400 S Monroe St, Tallahassee, FL 32399-0001

top_issues.name: Economic Development
top_issues.stance: Focused on job creation and business expansion in Florida.

top_issues.name: Tax Policy
top_issues.stance: Supports business-friendly tax policies and fiscal discipline.

top_issues.name: Infrastructure
top_issues.stance: Prioritizes transportation and infrastructure investment.

cabinet.name: James Uthmeier
cabinet.selection_detail: Appointed by Governor DeSantis on Feb 17, 2025 to fill the vacancy after Ashley Moody was appointed to the US Senate. The seat is normally elected statewide.

contact.dc_office: Office of the Attorney General, PL 01, The Capitol, 400 S Monroe St, Tallahassee, FL 32399-1050

top_issues.name: Law Enforcement
top_issues.stance: Committed to supporting law enforcement and public safety initiatives.

top_issues.name: Crime Prevention
top_issues.stance: Focuses on prosecution of crime and victim protection.

top_issues.name: Constitutional Rights
top_issues.stance: Defends constitutional rights and rule of law.

cabinet.name: Blaise Ingoglia
cabinet.selection_detail: Appointed by Governor DeSantis on July 17, 2025 to fill the vacancy after Jimmy Patronis resigned to run for U.S. Congress. The seat is normally elected statewide.

contact.dc_office: Department of Financial Services, 200 E Gaines St, Tallahassee, FL 32399-0301

top_issues.name: Financial Management
top_issues.stance: Emphasizes fiscal responsibility and efficient budget management.

top_issues.name: Insurance Reform
top_issues.stance: Advocates for homeowners insurance market stability.

top_issues.name: Taxpayer Protection
top_issues.stance: Committed to protecting taxpayer interests and state funds.

cabinet.name: Wilton Simpson
cabinet.selection_detail: Elected statewide in November 2022 to a 4-year term.

contact.dc_office: Florida Department of Agriculture & Consumer Services, 407 S Calhoun St, Tallahassee, FL 32399-0800

leadership.bio: Ben Albritton represents parts of Charlotte, DeSoto, Glades, Hardee, Hendry, Highlands, Okeechobee, and Polk counties.

judiciary._note: Florida judiciary (demo data). Supreme Court and District Courts of Appeal judges are appointed by the Governor from Judicial Nominating Commission slates, then face merit-retention votes every 6 years. Circuit and County judges are elected in non-partisan elections; mid-term vacancies are filled by gubernatorial appointment.

members.name: Carlos G. Muñiz
members.selection_detail: Appointed by Governor DeSantis on Jan 22, 2019. Retained by voters in 2020 and last faced merit retention in 2022. Selected Chief Justice by the Court on a rotating basis starting July 2024.

contact.dc_office: Supreme Court of Florida, Supreme Court Building, 500 S Duval St, Tallahassee, FL 32399-1927

top_issues.name: Judicial Independence
top_issues.stance: Committed to protecting the independence and integrity of the judiciary.

top_issues.name: Case Management
top_issues.stance: Focused on fair and efficient case management and court operations.

top_issues.name: Rule of Law
top_issues.stance: Dedicated to fair application of law and judicial precedent.

members.name: Charles T. Canady
members.selection_detail: Appointed by Governor Crist in 2008. Served as Chief Justice 2010-12, 2018-22, 2022-24. Most recently retained by voters in 2022.
